name: DataGrid Control
description: Display collections of data with sortable, resizable columns

content.title: DataGrid Control
content.content:
# DataGrid Control

## Purpose

The DataGrid control displays collections of data in a tabular format with sortable, resizable columns and optional virtualization for large datasets.

## Basic Usage

### Example 1: Simple DataGrid

```csharp --render DataGridExample1 --show-code
public record DataRecord(string SystemName, string DisplayName);

var data = new DataRecord[] 
{ 
    new("1", "First"), 
    new("2", "Second"),
    new("3", "Third")
};

new DataGridControl(data)
    .WithColumn(
        new PropertyColumnControl<string>
        {
            Property = "systemName",
            Title = "System Name"
        }
    )
    .WithColumn(
        new PropertyColumnControl<string>
        {
            Property = "displayName",
            Title = "Display Name"
        }
    )
```

**What you see above**:
- A table with 2 columns and 3 rows
- First column shows "System Name" header with values "1", "2", "3"
- Second column shows "Display Name" header with values "First", "Second", "Third"
- Columns are sortable and resizable by default
- Try clicking column headers to sort

### Example 2: Auto-Generated Grid

```csharp --render DataGridExample2 --show-code
public record Person
{
    public string Name { get; init; }
    public int Age { get; init; }
}

var people = new[] 
{ 
    new Person { Name = "Alice", Age = 30 },
    new Person { Name = "Bob", Age = 25 },
    new Person { Name = "Charlie", Age = 35 }
};

new DataGridControl(people)
    .WithColumn(new PropertyColumnControl<string> { Property = "name", Title = "Name" })
    .WithColumn(new PropertyColumnControl<int> { Property = "age", Title = "Age" })
```

**What you see above**:
- Automatically creates columns for all public properties
- Column headers derived from property names ("Name", "Age")
- Columns are sortable and resizable
- Try sorting by Age or Name

## Column Types

### PropertyColumnControl

Displays a property value from each row item.

**Configuration**:
```csharp
new PropertyColumnControl<string>
{
    Property = "name",           // Property name (camelCase)
    Title = "Full Name",         // Column header
    Sortable = true,             // Enable sorting (default)
    Resizable = true,            // Enable resizing (default)
    Width = "200px",             // Fixed width
    Align = "center"             // Text alignment
}
```

### TemplateColumnControl

Renders custom controls for each cell.

**Configuration**:
```csharp
new TemplateColumnControl<Person>
{
    Title = "Actions",
    CellTemplate = person => Controls.Button($"Edit {person.Name}")
}
```

## Grid Configuration

### Virtualization

Enables efficient rendering of large datasets.

**Input**:
```csharp
var grid = new DataGridControl(largeDataset)
    .WithVirtualize(true)
    .WithItemSize(50);           // Row height in pixels
```

**Result**: Only visible rows are rendered in the DOM. Scrolling smoothly loads rows as needed.

### Pagination

**Input**:
```csharp
var grid = new DataGridControl(data)
    .WithPagination(true)
    .WithItemsPerPage(10)
    .WithShowPageSizeSelector(true)
    .WithPageSizeOptions(new[] { 5, 10, 25, 50 });
```

**Result**: Data is split into pages with navigation controls and page size selector.

### Resizable Columns

**Input**:
```csharp
var grid = new DataGridControl(data)
    .Resizable(true);            // Enable column resizing
```

**Result**: Users can drag column borders to adjust widths.

### Column Sorting

Each column is sortable by default.

**Disable sorting**:
```csharp
new PropertyColumnControl<string>
{
    Property = "id",
    Sortable = false             // Disable sorting for this column
}
```

## Column Configuration

### Width Control

| Property | Purpose | Example |
|----------|---------|----------|
| `Width` | Fixed width | `"200px"`, `"15%"` |
| `MinWidth` | Minimum width | `"100px"` |
| `MaxWidth` | Maximum width | `"400px"` |

**Example**:
```csharp
new PropertyColumnControl<string>
{
    Property = "description",
    MinWidth = "150px",
    MaxWidth = "500px"
}
```

### Alignment

| Property | Purpose | Values |
|----------|---------|--------|
| `Align` | Cell content alignment | `"start"`, `"center"`, `"end"` |
| `HeaderAlign` | Header text alignment | `"start"`, `"center"`, `"end"` |

**Example**:
```csharp
new PropertyColumnControl<int>
{
    Property = "amount",
    Align = "end",               // Right-align numbers
    HeaderAlign = "end"
}
```

### Visibility

**Hide column conditionally**:
```csharp
new PropertyColumnControl<string>
{
    Property = "internalId",
    Visible = false              // Column exists but is hidden
}
```

## Row Configuration

### Row Styling

**Input**:
```csharp
var grid = new DataGridControl(data)
    .WithShowHover(true)         // Highlight row on hover
    .WithRowClass("custom-row")  // CSS class for rows
    .WithRowStyle("border: 1px solid gray");
```

**Result**: Rows have custom styling and hover effects.

### Row Selection

**Input**:
```csharp
var grid = new DataGridControl(data)
    .WithSelectionMode("single");  // "single" or "multiple"
```

**Result**: Users can select rows. Selected items are tracked in the grid state.

## Empty State

**Input**:
```csharp
var grid = new DataGridControl(emptyList)
    .WithEmptyContent(Controls.Label("No data available"));
```

**Result**: When the data collection is empty, the custom empty content is displayed.

## Loading State

**Input**:
```csharp
var grid = new DataGridControl(data)
    .WithLoading(true)
    .WithLoadingTemplate(Controls.Label("Loading data..."));
```

**Result**: Grid displays loading template while data is being fetched.

## Common Patterns

### Data Table with Actions

```csharp --render DataGridActions --show-code
public record User(string Name, string Email);

var users = new[]
{
    new User("Alice Smith", "alice@example.com"),
    new User("Bob Jones", "bob@example.com"),
    new User("Charlie Brown", "charlie@example.com")
};

new DataGridControl(users)
    .WithColumn(new PropertyColumnControl<string> 
    { 
        Property = "name", 
        Title = "Name" 
    })
    .WithColumn(new PropertyColumnControl<string> 
    { 
        Property = "email", 
        Title = "Email" 
    })
    .WithColumn(new TemplateColumnControl<User>
    {
        Title = "Actions",
        CellTemplate = user => Controls.Stack
            .WithOrientation(Orientation.Horizontal)
            .WithArea(Controls.Button("Edit"))
            .WithArea(Controls.Button("Delete"))
    })
```

Shows a grid with custom action buttons in each row.

### Paginated Report

```csharp --render DataGridPaginated --show-code
public record ReportItem(string Id, string Status, int Value);

var reportData = Enumerable.Range(1, 50)
    .Select(i => new ReportItem(
        $"ID-{i}",
        i % 2 == 0 ? "Active" : "Inactive",
        i * 100
    ))
    .ToArray();

new DataGridControl(reportData)
    .WithPagination(true)
    .WithItemsPerPage(10)
    .WithShowPageSizeSelector(true)
    .Resizable(true)
```

Shows a paginated grid with 50 items, 10 per page. Try changing the page size and navigating pages.

### Compact Grid

```csharp --render DataGridCompact --show-code
public record CompactItem(string Code, string Description);

var data = Enumerable.Range(1, 20)
    .Select(i => new CompactItem($"C{i}", $"Description {i}"))
    .ToArray();

new DataGridControl(data)
    .WithItemSize(35)
    .WithVirtualize(true)
    .WithGenerateHeader("Sticky")
```

Shows a compact grid with smaller row height and sticky header. Scroll to see the header stay visible.

## Performance Considerations

**Large Datasets (1000+ rows)**:
- Enable virtualization: `.WithVirtualize(true)`
- Set appropriate item size: `.WithItemSize(50)`
- Consider pagination: `.WithPagination(true)`

**Many Columns**:
- Set `GridTemplateColumns` for better control
- Hide less important columns with `Visible = false`
- Use `MinWidth` to prevent columns from becoming too narrow
